text: Alice is married under section 7703 for the year 2017. Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2017. Alice's and her spouse's taxable income for the year 2017 is $17330.
question: Alice and her spouse have to pay $2600 in taxes for the year 2017 under section 1(a)(i).
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Tax computation under section 1(a) - married individuals filing joint returns and surviving spouses */
tax_section_1a(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ;
        Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

taxable_income(alice, 17330).
married_under_7703(alice).
files_joint_return(alice).

/* ---------- Derivation of Alice and spouse's tax liability ---------- */

alice_and_spouse_tax(Tax) :-
    taxable_income(alice, TI),
    married_under_7703(alice),
    files_joint_return(alice),
    tax_section_1a(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  alice_and_spouse_tax(Tax),
      round(Tax, Rounded),
      Rounded =:= 2600
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

taxable_income(alice, 95129).

alice_tax(Tax) :-

   ).

text: Alice's taxable income for the year 2017 is $22895. Alice is not married, is not a surviving spouse, and is not a head of household in 2017.
question: Alice has to pay $3538 in taxes for the year 2017 under section 1(c)(iii).
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Tax computation under section 1(c) - unmarried individuals (other than surviving spouses and heads of household) */
tax_section_1c(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000)
    ;
        Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

taxable_income(alice, 22895).
not_married(alice).
not_surviving_spouse(alice).
not_head_of_household(alice).

/* ---------- Marital status for Alice (derived from facts) ---------- */

/* Under section 7703(a): marital status is determined as of the close of the taxable year. */
married_under_7703(_Person) :- fail.

/* ---------- Classification for Alice ---------- */

/* Section 1(c) applies when an individual is not married, not a surviving spouse, and not a head of household. */
unmarried_individual_other_than_surviving_spouse_or_head_of_household(Person) :-
    \+ married_under_7703(Person),
    not_surviving_spouse(Person),
    not_head_of_household(Person).

/* ---------- Derivation of Alice's tax liability ---------- */

alice_tax(Tax) :-
    taxable_income(alice, TI),
    unmarried_individual_other_than_surviving_spouse_or_head_of_household(alice),
    tax_section_1c(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

claim_tax(3538).

:- (  alice_tax(Tax),
      claim_tax(ClaimTax),
      Tax =:= ClaimTax
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

In 2017, Bob earned $5254312.

reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Tax computation under section 1(a) - married individuals filing jointly and surviving spouses */
tax_section_1a(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ;
        Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

taxable_income(alice, 236422).
surviving_spouse(alice).   % Alice is a surviving spouse for 2017

/* ---------- Derivation of Alice's tax liability ---------- */

alice_tax(Tax) :-
    taxable_income(alice, TI),
    surviving_spouse(alice),
    tax_section_1a(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  alice_tax(Tax),
      round(Tax, Rounded),
      Rounded =:= 70640
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice and Bob have a child, Charlie, born October 9th, 2000. Alice died on July 9th, 2014. From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. Bob is entitled to a deduction for Charlie under section 151(c) for the years 2015 to 2019.
question: Section 2(b)(1)(B) applies to Bob in 2018.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

% Marriage and child
married(alice, bob).
spouse(alice, bob).
spouse(bob, alice).

child(charlie, bob).

% Alice died on July 9, 2014 (during 2014)
death_year(alice, 2014).

% Bob maintained the household (costs > 50%) for the relevant period
maintains_household(bob, Year) :- integer(Year), Year >= 2004, Year =< 2019.

% Bob is entitled to a deduction for Charlie under section 151(c) for 2015-2019
deduction_for(bob, charlie, Year) :- integer(Year), Year >= 2015, Year =< 2019.

% Bob is not a nonresident alien (relevant for section 2(b)(3)(A))
nonresident_alien(bob, Year) :- fail.

/* ---------- Statutory definitions (encoded as predicates) ---------- */

% §7703(a): At close of the taxable year, Bob is married if his spouse is alive then.
married_at_close_of_year(Person, Year) :-
    spouse(Person, Spouse),
    \+ death_year(Spouse, Year).

% §7703(c): Special rule for certain married individuals living apart (not needed here)
% We encode it as always failing for this simplified case.
married_individual_living_apart(Person, Year) :- fail.

% §2(b)(2)(C): If spouse died during the taxable year, individual is considered married at close.
considered_married_due_to_spouse_death(Person, Year) :-
    spouse(Person, Spouse),
    death_year(Spouse, Year).

% §2(b)(2)(A)-(B): Legal separation / nonresident spouse rules (not used here)
considered_not_married_due_to_legal_separation(Person, Year) :- fail.
considered_not_married_due_to_nonresident_spouse(Person, Year) :- fail.

% §2(b)(1): Head of household requires not married and not a surviving spouse.
% We also encode the "maintains household" condition in (1)(A) and (1)(B).
head_of_household(Person, Year) :-
    \+ ( considered_married_due_to_spouse_death(Person, Year)
       ; married_individual_living_apart(Person, Year)
       ; married_at_close_of_year(Person, Year)
       ; considered_not_married_due_to_legal_separation(Person, Year)
       ; considered_not_married_due_to_nonresident_spouse(Person, Year)
       ),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year),
    maintains_household(Person, Year),
    (
        % §2(b)(1)(A): Principal place of abode of a qualifying child (or other dependent)
        ( qualifies_as_qualifying_child(charlie, bob, Year),
          principal_place_abode(charlie, bob, Year),
          entitled_to_deduction(bob, charlie, Year)
        )
    ;
        % §2(b)(1)(B): Principal place of abode of father/mother (not used here)
        fail
    ).

% §2(b)(3)(A): Head of household cannot be a nonresident alien
head_of_household(Person, Year) :-
    nonresident_alien(Person, Year),
    !,
    fail.

% §2(a)(1): Surviving spouse definition
surviving_spouse(Person, Year) :-
    spouse(Person, DeceasedSpouse),
    death_year(DeceasedSpouse, DeathYear),
    ( DeathYear =:= Year - 1 ; DeathYear =:= Year - 2 ),
    maintains_household(Person, Year),
    entitled_to_deduction(Person, ChildDependent, Year),
    dependent_relationship(ChildDependent, Person, son_or_daughter),
    \+ remarried_before_close_of_year(Person, Year),
    joint_return_possible_for_death_year(Person, DeathYear).

% §2(a)(2)(A): No surviving spouse if remarried before close of year
remarried_before_close_of_year(Person, Year) :- fail.

% §2(a)(2)(B): Joint return possible for the year spouse died (simplified)
joint_return_possible_for_death_year(Person, DeathYear) :-
    spouse(Person, _),
    DeathYear =:= DeathYear.

% §152(c): Qualifying child (subset sufficient for this case)
qualifies_as_qualifying_child(Child, Parent, Year) :-
    child(Child, Parent),
    same_principal_place_abode(Child, Parent, Year),
    age_less_than_25(Child, Year),
    \+ filed_joint_return_with_spouse(Child, Year).

same_principal_place_abode(Child, Parent, Year) :-
    principal_place_abode(Child, Parent, Year).

age_less_than_25(Child, Year) :-
    birth_year(Child, BirthYear),
    Age is Year - BirthYear,
    Age < 25.

filed_joint_return_with_spouse(Child, Year) :- fail.

% §152(c)(2)(A): Child relationship
dependent_relationship(Child, Parent, son_or_daughter) :-
    child(Child, Parent).

% §151(c): Entitled to a deduction for a dependent (subset sufficient for this case)
entitled_to_deduction(Person, Dependent, Year) :-
    deduction_for(Person, Dependent, Year).

% Household/principal place of abode facts (derived from case facts)
principal_place_abode(charlie, bob, Year) :-
    maintains_household(bob, Year).

% Birth year (from case facts)
birth_year(charlie, 2000).

/* ---------- Claim-specific check ---------- */

claim_statement :-
    head_of_household(bob, 2018).

/* ---------- Query and output ---------- */

:- (  claim_statement
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice died on July 9th, 2014.
question: Section 2(b)(2)(C) applies to Bob in 2014.

/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Tax computation under section 1(c)(iii) - unmarried individuals, bracket (iii) */
tax_section_1c_iii(TI, Tax) :-
    % Section 1(c)(iii) applies if taxable income is over $53,500 but not over $115,000
    TI > 53500,
    TI =< 115000,
    Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

taxable_income(alice, 22895).
unmarried(alice).
not_surviving_spouse(alice).
not_head_of_household(alice).

/* ---------- Derivation of Alice's tax liability under 1(c)(iii) ---------- */

alice_tax_under_1c_iii(Tax) :-
    taxable_income(alice, TI),
    unmarried(alice),
    not_surviving_spouse(alice),
    not_head_of_household(alice),
    tax_section_1c_iii(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  alice_tax_under_1c_iii(Tax),
      round(Tax, Rounded),
      Rounded =:= 3538
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: In 2017, Alice was paid $33200. Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2017, and they file a joint return for 2017.
question: Section 63(c)(2)(A)(ii) applies to Alice in 2017.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Standard deduction for various marital statuses */
standard_deduction(joint_or_surviving_spouse, 6000).
standard_deduction(head_of_household, 4400).
standard_deduction(other, 3000).

/* Check if the standard deduction rule applies to Alice */
applies_to_alice(standard_deduction_rule) :-
    standard_deduction_rule = standard_deduction(joint_or_surviving_spouse, _),
    alice_marital_status(joint_or_surviving_spouse).

alice_marital_status(joint_or_surviving_spouse) :-
    alice_married(true),
    alice_files_joint_return(true).

/* Facts for the case */
alice_married(true).
alice_files_joint_return(true).

/* Verification of the claim */
:- (  applies_to_alice(standard_deduction(joint_or_surviving_spouse, _))
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: In 2017, Alice was paid $33200. Alice was born March 2nd, 1950 and Bob was born March 3rd, 1955.
question: Under section 63(f)(3), Alice's additional standard deduction in 2017 is equal to $750.

text: In 2016, Alice's income was $277192. Alice is a head of household for the year 2016. Alice is allowed itemized deductions of $60000 under section 63.
question: Section 68(a)(2) prescribes a reduction of Alice's itemized deductions for the year 2016 by $48000.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules needed for this case ---------- */

/* Applicable amount for a head of household under §68(b)(1)(B) */
applicable_amount(head_of_household, 275000).

/* Compute the reduction under §68(a)(2) when it applies */
section_68_reduction(Itemized, AGI, Reduction) :-
    applicable_amount(head_of_household, ApplicableAmount),
    Excess is AGI - ApplicableAmount,
    Excess > 0,
    Reduction is 0.8 * Itemized.

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

status(alice, head_of_household).
agi(alice, 277192).
itemized_deductions(alice, 60000).

/* ---------- Derive the reduction for Alice ---------- */

alice_reduction(Reduction) :-
    status(alice, Status),
    agi(alice, AGI),
    itemized_deductions(alice, Itemized),
    section_68_reduction(Itemized, AGI, Reduction).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  alice_reduction(Reduction),
      round(Reduction, Rounded),
      Rounded =:= 48000
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: In 2016, Alice's income was $567192. Alice is a head of household for the year 2016.
question: Section 68(b)(1)(C) applies to Alice in 2016.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (relevant portions) ---------- */

/* §68(b)(1): Applicable amount */
applicable_amount(joint_return, 300000).
applicable_amount(surviving_spouse, 300000).
applicable_amount(head_of_household, 275000).
applicable_amount(single_not_head_survivor, 250000).

/* §68(b)(1)(C): the applicable amount for a single individual who is not a surviving spouse or head of household is $250,000 */
applicable_amount_b1c(SingleNotHeadSurvivor) :-
    SingleNotHeadSurvivor = single_not_head_survivor,
    applicable_amount(SingleNotHeadSurvivor, 250000).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */
person(alice).
year(2016).

income(alice, 2016, 567192).

/* Alice is a head of household for the year 2016 */
marital_status_for_tax(alice, 2016, head_of_household).

/* ---------- Derived classification (not strictly needed here, but included for clarity) ---------- */
tax_filing_status(Person, Year, Status) :-
    marital_status_for_tax(Person, Year, Status).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */
claim_applies_to_alice_in_2016 :-
    tax_filing_status(alice, 2016, head_of_household),
    applicable_amount_b1c(head_of_household).

:- (  claim_applies_to_alice_in_2016
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Alice and Bob have been married since 2 Feb 2015. Bob has no income for 2015.
question: Alice can receive an exemption for Bob under section 151(b) for the year 2015.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Rule for exemption for spouse under section 151(b) */
spouse_exemption_allowed(Taxpayer, Spouse) :-
    marital_status(Taxpayer, married),
    not_joint_return(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    no_gross_income(Spouse),
    not_dependent_of_another(Spouse).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

marital_status(alice, married).
married_to(alice, bob).
no_gross_income(bob).
not_joint_return(alice, bob).
not_dependent_of_another(bob).

/* ---------- Derivation of Alice's eligibility for Bob's exemption ---------- */

alice_eligibility_for_bob_exemption :-
    married_to(alice, bob),
    spouse_exemption_allowed(alice, bob).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  alice_eligibility_for_bob_exemption
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Alice has a son, Bob, who satisfies section 152(c)(1) for the year 2015.
question: Under section 152(a), Bob is a dependent of Alice for the year 2015.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rule: section 2(a)(2)(B) ---------- */
/* A joint return could have been made unless either spouse is a nonresident alien */
joint_return_could_have_been_made(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    spouse(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    \+ nonresident_alien(Taxpayer, Year),
    \+ nonresident_alien(Spouse, Year).

/* Spouse died during the taxable year */
spouse_died_in_year(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    spouse(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    died(Spouse, Year).

/* Section 2(a)(2)(B) applies if a joint return could have been made
   for the taxpayer's taxable year during which the spouse died */
section_2a2b_applies(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    spouse_died_in_year(Taxpayer, Year),
    joint_return_could_have_been_made(Taxpayer, Year).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */
spouse(bob, alice).
spouse(alice, bob).

died(alice, 2014).

nonresident_alien(alice, 2014).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */
:- (  section_2a2b_applies(bob, 2014)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Alice has a son, Bob, who satisfies section 152(c)(1) for the year 2015. Bob has a son, Charlie, who satisfies section 152(c)(1) for the year 2015. Alice's income in 2015 was $504598. Bob had no income in 2015.
question: Section 152(b)(1) applies to Alice for the year 2015.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 152(b)(1): An individual cannot be a dependent if they are already a dependent of another taxpayer */
dependent_of(another_taxpayer, individual) :-
    satisfies_qualifying_child(individual, another_taxpayer).

cannot_be_dependent(individual) :-
    dependent_of(_, individual).

/* Section 152(c)(1): Defines a qualifying child */
satisfies_qualifying_child(child, parent) :-
    relationship(child, parent),
    residency(child, parent),
    age_requirements(child).

/* Relationship, residency, and age requirements for a qualifying child */
relationship(bob, alice).
residency(bob, alice).
age_requirements(bob).

relationship(charlie, bob).
residency(charlie, bob).
age_requirements(charlie).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

income(alice, 504598).
income(bob, 0).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

alice_cannot_be_dependent :-
    dependent_of(alice, bob),
    cannot_be_dependent(alice).

/* Check the claim */
:- (  alice_cannot_be_dependent
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Alice has a son, Bob. From September 1st, 2015 to November 3rd, 2019, Alice and Bob lived in the same home. Bob married Charlie on October 23rd, 2018. Bob satisfied section 152(c)(2) and 152(c)(3) with Alice as the taxpayer for the years 2015 to 2020.
question: Under section 152(c)(1), Bob is a qualifying child of Alice for the year 2019.

text: Alice has a brother, Bob, who was born January 31st, 2014.
question: Bob bears a relationship to Alice under section 152(c)(2)(A).
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (relevant excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 2(b)(1): Head of household definition */
head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    not(married_at_end_of_year(Taxpayer, Year)),
    not(surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year)),
    (
        (
            maintains_household_for_period(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year, Period),
            qualifying_child(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year),
            Period > 0.5
        )
        ;
        (
            maintains_household_for_period(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year, _),
            dependent_of_taxpayer(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year),
            entitled_to_deduction(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year),
            father_or_mother(Dependent, Taxpayer)
        )
    ),
    furnished_over_half_cost(Taxpayer, Year).

/* Section 2(b)(1)(B): Maintains household as principal place of abode for father or mother, deduction allowed under section 151 */
section_2b1B_applies(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    maintains_household_for_period(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year, _),
    principal_place_of_abode(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year),
    father_or_mother(Dependent, Taxpayer),
    entitled_to_deduction(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year),
    furnished_over_half_cost(Taxpayer, Year).

/* Relationship: father_or_mother/2 is true if Dependent is the father or mother of Taxpayer. */

/* ---------- Case facts ---------- */

/* Family structure */
married(alice, bob, 1992).
child_of(charlie, alice).
child_of(charlie, bob).
born(charlie, date(2000,10,9)).
died(alice, date(2014,7,9)).

/* Bob and Charlie lived together; Bob furnished household costs from 2004 to 2019 */
furnished_over_half_cost(bob, Year) :- Year >= 2004, Year =< 2019.
lived_together(bob, charlie, Year) :- Year >= 2004, Year =< 2019.
principal_place_of_abode(charlie, bob, Year) :- lived_together(bob, charlie, Year).
maintains_household_for_period(bob, charlie, Year, 1.0) :- lived_together(bob, charlie, Year).

/* Bob entitled to deduction for Charlie under section 151(c) for 2015-2019 */
entitled_to_deduction(bob, charlie, Year) :- Year >= 2015, Year =< 2019.

/* Charlie is Bob's son, so not his father/mother */
father_or_mother(X, Y) :- fail.  /* No such relationship for Bob and Charlie */

/* Section 2(b)(1)(B) reference: checks for head of household via maintaining home for father/mother with deduction */
dependent_of_taxpayer(charlie, bob, Year) :- entitled_to_deduction(bob, charlie, Year).

/* Marital/surviving spouse status for Bob in 2018 */

/* Alice died in 2014, so for years 2015+ Bob is not a surviving spouse (surviving spouse only for 2 years after death) */
surviving_spouse(bob, Year) :- alice_died_year(DeathYear), (Year =:= DeathYear; Year =:= DeathYear + 1), maintains_household_for_dependent(bob, Year).
alice_died_year(2014).

maintains_household_for_dependent(bob, Year) :-
    maintains_household_for_period(bob, Dep, Year, Period),
    Period > 0,
    entitled_to_deduction(bob, Dep, Year),
    son_or_daughter(Dep, bob).

son_or_daughter(Dep, Taxpayer) :- child_of(Dep, Taxpayer).

/* Marital status: Alice died in 2014, so Bob is unmarried after that; no evidence of remarriage */
married_at_end_of_year(bob, Year) :- Year =< 2014.

/* ---------- Query to test if section 2(b)(1)(B) applies to Bob in 2018 ---------- */

% Section 2(b)(1)(B) requires Bob to maintain as principal abode the household for his father or mother (for whom he is entitled to a deduction).
% In the facts, Bob maintains for his son Charlie, not for his father or mother.

:- (  section_2b1B_applies(bob, 2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Alice has a son, Bob, who was born January 31st, 2014.
question: Alice bears a relationship to Bob under section 152(c)(2).
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 2(b)(2)(C):
   "a taxpayer shall be considered as married at the close of his taxable year if his spouse (other than a spouse described in subparagraph (B)) died during the taxable year."
*/

/* For 2(b)(2)(C) to apply to Bob in 2014:
   - Bob's spouse (Alice) died during Bob's taxable year (2014).
   - Alice was not a nonresident alien (not indicated, assume not).
*/

/* Facts from the case */
married(alice, bob, date(1992,2,3)).
died(alice, date(2014,7,9)).
taxable_year(bob, 2014).

/* For this scenario, we assume the taxable year is the calendar year.
   Bob's spouse Alice died during 2014.
*/

/* Section 2(b)(2)(C) applies to Bob in 2014 if:
   - During Bob's taxable year 2014, his spouse died.
   - The spouse was not a nonresident alien (no evidence she was).
*/

spouse(X,Y) :- married(X,Y, _).
spouse(Y,X) :- married(X,Y, _).

spouse_died_during_taxable_year(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    spouse(Spouse, Taxpayer),
    died(Spouse, date(Year, _, _)).

not_nonresident_alien(_). /* No evidence Alice was a nonresident alien. Assume true. */

section_2b2c_applies(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    spouse_died_during_taxable_year(Taxpayer, Year),
    not_nonresident_alien(_).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  section_2b2c_applies(bob, 2014)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: In 2015, Alice's income was $312. The exemption amount for Alice under section 151(d) for the year 2015 was $2000. Bob is Alice's father.
question: Section 152(d)(1)(D) applies to Bob for the year 2015.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 63(c)(2) - Basic standard deduction amounts for 2017 (before amendment in (7)):
   (A) 200% of $3,000 ($6,000) for a joint return or a surviving spouse (subparagraph (A))
   (B) $4,400 for a head of household (subparagraph (B))
   (C) $3,000 in any other case (subparagraph (C))

   (A)(i) a joint return
   (A)(ii) a surviving spouse (as defined in section 2(a))
*/

/* Section 63(c)(2)(A)(ii) applies if taxpayer is a surviving spouse under section 2(a) */
basic_standard_deduction_applies_63c2aii(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year).

/* A surviving spouse (section 2(a)):
   - whose spouse died during either of the two years immediately preceding the taxable year,
   - AND maintains a household that is principal abode of a child/stepchild/dependent (§2(a)(1)(B)),
   - AND has not remarried before close of the taxable year (§2(a)(2)(A)),
   - AND could have filed joint return for year of spouse's death (§2(a)(2)(B))
*/

/* In the case, Alice is married for the entire year and files a joint return.
   Her spouse did NOT die in 2015 or 2016 (the two years prior), nor in 2017.
   Therefore, Alice is NOT a surviving spouse for 2017.
*/

/* Facts from the case */
year(2017).
taxpayer(alice).
taxpayer(bob).
married(alice, bob, 2017).
files_joint_return(alice, bob, 2017).
paid(alice, 33200, 2017).

/* Alice's spouse (Bob) is alive for all of 2017 (married since Feb 3, 2017), so Alice is not a widow. */
spouse_died_before_year(_, _, _) :- fail.   /* No death */

/* Alice is not a surviving spouse for 2017 */
surviving_spouse(alice, 2017) :- fail.

/* Section 63(c)(2)(A)(ii) only applies if Alice is a surviving spouse. */
section_63c2aii_applies_to(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    basic_standard_deduction_applies_63c2aii(Taxpayer, Year).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  section_63c2aii_applies_to(alice, 2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Bob is Alice's brother since April 15th, 2014.
question: Alice bears a relationship to Bob under section 152(d)(2)(A).

text: Charlie is Alice's father since April 15th, 2014. Bob is Charlie's brother since October 12th, 1992.
question: Alice bears a relationship to Bob under section 152(d)(2)(D) for the year 2018.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 63(f): Additional standard deduction for aged */

/*
63(f)(1): The taxpayer shall be entitled to an additional amount of $600—
    (A) for himself if he has attained age 65 before the close of his taxable year,
    ...
63(f)(3): Higher amount for certain unmarried individuals:
    In the case of an individual who is not married and is not a surviving spouse,
    paragraphs (1) and (2) shall be applied by substituting "$750" for "$600".
*/

/* For 2017, an individual is considered age 65 if born on or before December 31, 1952. */

attained_age_65_in_year(DateOfBirth, Year) :-
    DateOfBirth = date(YearOfBirth, Month, Day),
    (YearOfBirth < Year - 65 ;
     YearOfBirth =:= Year - 65, Month =< 12, Day =< 31).

/* Marital/surviving spouse status is not specified in the case.
   Assume Alice is unmarried and not a surviving spouse for purposes of 63(f)(3).
   (If she was married, the rule would use $600, not $750.)
*/

/* Additional standard deduction calculation for aged (ignore blind for this case) */
additional_standard_deduction_aged(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :-
    date_of_birth(Taxpayer, DOB),
    attained_age_65_in_year(DOB, Year),
    not_married(Taxpayer, Year),
    not_surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year),
    Amount = 750.

/* Facts from the case */

date_of_birth(alice, date(1950,3,2)).
date_of_birth(bob, date(1955,3,3)).
paid(alice, 33200). /* Not relevant for additional standard deduction */
tax_year(2017).

/* Assume: Alice is not married and not a surviving spouse in 2017. */
not_married(alice, 2017).
not_surviving_spouse(alice, 2017).

/* ---------- Derivation of Alice's additional standard deduction ---------- */

alice_additional_standard_deduction_aged_2017(Amount) :-
    tax_year(2017),
    additional_standard_deduction_aged(alice, 2017, Amount).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  alice_additional_standard_deduction_aged_2017(Amount),
      Amount =:= 750
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Charlie is Bob's father since April 15th, 2014. Alice married Charlie on October 12th, 1992.
question: Alice bears a relationship to Bob under section 152(d)(2)(F).
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Relationship between individuals as defined in section 152(d)(2) */
relationship_to_taxpayer(Taxpayer, Individual) :-
    % F: A brother or sister of the father or mother of the taxpayer
    parent_of(Taxpayer, Parent),
    sibling_of(Parent, Individual).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

parent_of(charlie, bob).
married_to(alice, charlie, 'October 12th, 1992').

/* ---------- Helper predicates ---------- */

sibling_of(X, Y) :-
    parent_of(X, P),
    parent_of(Y, P),
    X \= Y.

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

alice_relationship_to_bob :-
    parent_of(charlie, bob),
    sibling_of(bob, Individual),
    relationship_to_taxpayer(alice, Individual).

/* ---------- Query to check the claim ---------- */

:- (  alice_relationship_to_bob
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Alice and Bob started living together on April 15th, 2014. Alice and Bob are not married.
question: Alice bears a relationship to Bob under section 152(d)(2)(H) for the year 2018.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 68(b)(1): Defines "applicable amount" for the limitation on itemized deductions */
applicable_amount(joint_return_or_surviving_spouse, 300000).
applicable_amount(head_of_household, 275000).
applicable_amount(other_individual, 250000).
applicable_amount(married_separate, 150000).

/* Section 68(b)(1)(A)-(D): Identifies which marital/status category a taxpayer falls into.
    (A) $300,000: joint return or surviving spouse (as defined in section 2(a))
    (B) $275,000: head of household (as defined in section 2(b))
    (C) $250,000: individual who is not married and who is not a surviving spouse or head of household
    (D) half of (A): married individual filing separate return
*/

/* Statutory rule: For section 68(b)(1)(C) to apply, the individual must be 
   "not married and ... not a surviving spouse or head of household".
*/
section_68_b_1_c_applies(Taxpayer) :-
    not(married_under_7703(Taxpayer)),
    not(surviving_spouse(Taxpayer)),
    not(head_of_household(Taxpayer)).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

year(2016).
income(alice, 567192).
head_of_household(alice).
/* No indication that Alice is married or a surviving spouse. But the case explicitly states Alice is a head of household for 2016. */

/* For completeness, define negated facts for married and surviving spouse based on the information. */
not(married_under_7703(alice)).
not(surviving_spouse(alice)).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

/* The claim: Section 68(b)(1)(C) applies to Alice in 2016. */
claim_section_68_b_1_c_applies(alice) :- section_68_b_1_c_applies(alice).

:- (  claim_section_68_b_1_c_applies(alice)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

/* ---------- Statutory rules ---------- */

/* Predicate to check if Alice qualifies as an employer under Section 3306(a)(2)(B) */
is_employer(Alice) :-
    findall(Day, employed_on_day(Alice, Day, 5), Days),
    length(Days, Length),
    Days = [Day1, Day2, Day3, Day4, Day5, Day6, Day7, Day8, Day9, Day10 | _],
    distinct_weeks([Day1, Day2, Day3, Day4, Day5, Day6, Day7, Day8, Day9, Day10]).

/* Predicate to check if the days are in 10 different calendar weeks */
distinct_weeks(Days) :-
    maplist(week_of_year, Days, Weeks),
    sort(Weeks, SortedWeeks),
    length(SortedWeeks, 10).

/* Function to extract the week of the year from a date */
week_of_year(Date, Week) :-
    % Assuming Date is in the form of 'YYYY-MM-DD'
    sub_string(Date, 0, 4, _, Year),
    sub_string(Date, 5, 2, _, Month),
    sub_string(Date, 8, 2, _, Day),
    date_time_stamp(Year, Month, Day, TimeStamp),
    get_time(TimeStamp),
    get_time(TimeStamp),
    stamp_date_time(TimeStamp, DateTime, 'UTC'),
    date_time_value(week_of_year, DateTime, Week).

/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Definition of employer under section 3306(a)(2)(B) */
is_employer_agricultural_labor(Individual, Year) :-
    between(1, 10, Days),
    findall(Employed, employed_for_agricultural_labor(Individual, Employed, Year, Days), ListEmployed),
    length(ListEmployed, 5).

employed_for_agricultural_labor(Individual, Employed, Year, Days) :-
    employed(Individual, Employed, Year, Day),
    Day mod 7 =\= 0,
    between(1, Days, Day).

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

employed(alice, bob, 2017, 1).
employed(alice, cameron, 2017, 2).
employed(alice, dan, 2017, 3).
employed(alice, emily, 2017, 4).
employed(alice, fred, 2017, 5).
employed(alice, george, 2017, 6).

alice_is_employer_agricultural_labor :-
    is_employer_agricultural_labor(alice, 2017).

   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).

text: Alice has paid $3200 to Bob for agricultural labor done from Feb 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2017, in Caracas, Venezuela. Alice is an American employer.
question: Section 3306(c)(1) applies to Alice employing Bob for the year 2017.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 152(a): "dependent" means a qualifying child or qualifying relative */

dependent_of(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    qualifying_child_of(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year).

dependent_of(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    qualifying_relative_of(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year).

/* Section 152(d): qualifying relative */

/* Relationship test for qualifying relative under section 152(d)(2)
   (only relationships relevant to this case are modeled):
   - child or descendant of a child of the taxpayer
   - father or mother of the taxpayer, or an ancestor of either
*/

relationship_qr(Dependent, Taxpayer) :-
    % Dependent is a child of Taxpayer (152(d)(2)(A): child)
    parent(Taxpayer, Dependent).

relationship_qr(Dependent, Taxpayer) :-
    % Dependent is a descendant of a child of Taxpayer (grandchild, etc.)
    ancestor(Taxpayer, Dependent).

relationship_qr(Dependent, Taxpayer) :-
    % Dependent is father or mother of Taxpayer (152(d)(2)(C))
    parent(Dependent, Taxpayer).

relationship_qr(Dependent, Taxpayer) :-
    % Dependent is an ancestor of Taxpayer (e.g., grandparent)
    ancestor(Dependent, Taxpayer).

/* Ancestor relation (transitive closure of parent) */

ancestor(Ancestor, Descendant) :-
    parent(Ancestor, Descendant).

ancestor(Ancestor, Descendant) :-
    parent(Ancestor, Intermediate),
    ancestor(Intermediate, Descendant).

/* Income test for qualifying relative under section 152(d)(1)(B):
   "has no income" in the calendar year
*/

has_no_income(Person, Year) :-
    income(Person, Year, 0).

/* Full qualifying relative test for a given year (152(d)(1)):
   - bears a qualifying-relative relationship
   - has no income
   - is not a qualifying child of any taxpayer
*/

qualifying_relative_of(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    relationship_qr(Dependent, Taxpayer),
    has_no_income(Dependent, Year),
    \+ qualifying_child_of(Dependent, _, Year).

/* Section 152(b)(1): Dependents ineligible to have dependents.
   It "applies" to an individual in a given year if that individual
   is a dependent of some taxpayer for a taxable year beginning in
   that calendar year.
*/

section_152b1_applies(Person, Year) :-
    dependent_of(Person, _, Year).



/* ---------- Facts for the case (year 2015) ---------- */

/* Family relationships */
parent(alice, bob).
parent(bob, charlie).

/* Qualifying child facts as given in the case (Section 152(c)(1) satisfied) */
qualifying_child_of(bob, alice, 2015).
qualifying_child_of(charlie, bob, 2015).

/* Incomes for 2015 */
income(alice, 2015, 504598).
income(bob, 2015, 0).



/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */
/* Claim: Section 152(b)(1) applies to Alice for the year 2015. */

:- (  section_152b1_applies(alice, 2015)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Alice has paid wages of $3200 to Bob for domestic service done in her home from Feb 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2017, in Baltimore, Maryland, USA.
question: Section 3306(c)(2) applies to Alice employing Bob for the year 2017.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpt) ---------- */

/* §3306(c)(2): Domestic service in a private home, local college club, or local chapter of a college fraternity or sorority is excluded from "employment"
   unless performed for a person who paid cash remuneration of $1,000 or more to individuals employed in such domestic service in the calendar year
   or the preceding calendar year. */

statute_3306c2_excluded_from_employment(Employer, Year) :-
    % The service qualifies as "domestic service in a private home" (facts will establish that).
    domestic_service_in_private_home(Employer, Year),
    % Employer did NOT pay cash remuneration of $1,000 or more for domestic service in the relevant period.
    \+ paid_cash_for_domestic_service(Employer, Year, Amount),
    Amount >= 1000.

/* ---------- Facts for the case ---------- */

% The case concerns the calendar year 2017.
case_year(2017).

% Alice paid wages of $3200 to Bob for domestic service in her home during 2017.
paid_wages_to_alice_bob(2017, 3200).

% The service was domestic service performed in a private home.
domestic_service_in_private_home(alice, 2017).

% No other domestic-service payments are stated; therefore we encode the wage paid as the total cash paid for domestic service in the relevant period.
paid_cash_for_domestic_service(Employer, Year, Amount) :-
    paid_wages_to_alice_bob(Year, Amount),
    Employer = alice.

/* ---------- Derivation of whether §3306(c)(2) applies (i.e., excludes the service from "employment") ---------- */

section_3306c2_applies(Employer, Year) :-
    statute_3306c2_excluded_from_employment(Employer, Year).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  case_year(Year),
      section_3306c2_applies(alice, Year)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

text: Alice's father, Bob, was paid $3200 in 2017 for services performed for Johns Hopkins University. Alice was enrolled at Johns Hopkins University and attending classes from August 29, 2015 to May 30th, 2019.
question: Section 3306(c)(10)(A)(ii) applies to Bob's employment situation in 2017.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (relevant excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 152(c)(2): Relationship test for qualifying child */
relationship_152c2(Taxpayer, Child) :-
    % Child of the taxpayer (covers descendants as well in this simplified model)
    child_of(Child, Taxpayer).

/* Section 152(c)(3): Age requirements for qualifying child */
age_requirement_152c3(Taxpayer, Child, Year) :-
    % Directly use a fact that the age requirement is satisfied for the given year
    age_req_satisfied(Child, Taxpayer, Year).

/* Section 152(c)(1)(B): Same principal place of abode for more than one-half of the year */
same_abode_more_than_half_year(Taxpayer, Child, Year) :-
    principal_place_of_abode_same_more_than_half(Taxpayer, Child, Year).

/* Section 152(c)(1)(E): No disqualifying joint return with spouse */
% In this simplified model, this holds if there is no fact that the child filed
% a joint return with a spouse for that year (other than only for a claim of refund).
no_disqualifying_joint_return(Child, Year) :-
    \+ filed_joint_return_with_spouse_for_year(Child, Year).

/* Main definition: Section 152(c)(1) - Qualifying child */
qualifying_child_152c1(Taxpayer, Child, Year) :-
    relationship_152c2(Taxpayer, Child),
    same_abode_more_than_half_year(Taxpayer, Child, Year),
    age_requirement_152c3(Taxpayer, Child, Year),
    no_disqualifying_joint_return(Child, Year).

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

/* Alice has a son, Bob. */
child_of(bob, alice).

/* Bob satisfied section 152(c)(3) with Alice as the taxpayer for the years 2015 to 2020. */
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2015).
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2016).
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2017).
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2018).
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2019).
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2020).

/* From September 1st, 2015 to November 3rd, 2019, Alice and Bob lived in the same home.
   This implies that for 2019 they had the same principal place of abode for more than
   one-half of that taxable year. */
principal_place_of_abode_same_more_than_half(alice, bob, 2019).

/* Bob married Charlie on October 23rd, 2018. */
married(bob, charlie, date(2018,10,23)).

/* No fact is given that Bob and Charlie filed a joint return for 2019.
   We model this by making any such query fail. */
filed_joint_return_with_spouse_for_year(_, _) :- fail.

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  qualifying_child_152c1(alice, bob, 2019)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
